[According to the Amarakosa the tree is Rohitaka. It is other-

Should there be an ant-hill to the east of an Indra tree (Kahua),
there would be a vein of water one cubit to its west at a depth of 70
cubits. The indication is that at a depth of 6 cubits there would be
a brown alligator,
[According to the Amara (11-46) ^7%% is the same as and
Arjuna. Ksirasvamin states that Indradru has three meanings : Kutaja
Arjuna and Devadaru. It is possible that in the present context it
stands for Devadaru which grows on the hills.]

If there is an ant-hill to the north of a Suvaraaka tree [Cassia
fistula), there will be brackish water 75 cubits below at a distance
of two cubits to the south of the tree. When the earth is dug 2|-cubits
;
there will be found a mongoose; next copper-coloured stone; then,
red earth; then there flows a southerly vein.
